steps.title: Square both sides
steps.content:
Both sides are square roots, so square both sides:
$$
\sqrt{(x-2)^2}=\sqrt{3x+34}
$$
$$
(x-2)^2=3x+34
$$

steps.title: Calculator shortcut
steps.content: Graph the original equation as written to see the two intersection points. The algebra below explains why both candidates appear before selecting the smaller one.

steps.title: Factor the quadratic
steps.content:
Expand and move all terms to one side:
$$
x^2-4x+4=3x+34
$$
$$
x^2-7x-30=0
$$
$$
(x-10)(x+3)=0
$$
So the possible solutions are $x=10$ and $x=-3$.

steps.title: Calculator shortcut
steps.content: The graph shows both solutions, and the question asks for the smaller one. The answer is -3.
